channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: 'A completely different story': 300 million-year-old fossils reveal the first vertebrate land dwellers weren't what we thought, researchers claim - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'A completely different story': 300 million-year-old fossils reveal the first vertebrate land dwellers weren't what we thought, researchers claim  Live Science
  2. A Vietnam Veteran Collected Fossils for 66 Years. One, Mislabeled 'Baby Lamprey,' Made Paleontologists Reconsider How Vertebrates Moved From Water to Land  Smithsonian Magazine
  3. A textbook assumption about early land vertebrates may be wrong  Science News
  4. Fossilized babies of ancient crocodile-like predators uproot understanding of how animals adapted to the land  Phys.org
  5. A New Fossil Discovery Just Rewrote 150 Years of Evolutionary Theory  404 Media

item.title: The Sun may not engulf Earth after all, scientists say - Yahoo
item.description:
1. The Sun may not engulf Earth after all, scientists say  Yahoo
  2. Complex life on Earth may last 500 million years longer than expected  New Scientist
  3. Earth might escape the Sun’s fiery death – Scientists  The Sun Malaysia
  4. The real reason Earth escapes the dying sun gravity  The News International
  5. Good news—we have extra time before the Sun ends life on Earth  Ars Technica

item.title: In June 2026, Relativity Space — the rocket company Eric Schmidt took over in 2025, whose one rocket has never reached orbit — said it would privately build and fly a Mars orbiter in 2028, carrying NASA's Aeolus atmospheric instruments and doubling as a c - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In June 2026, Relativity Space — the rocket company Eric Schmidt took over in 2025, whose one rocket has never reached orbit — said it would privately build and fly a Mars orbiter in 2028, carrying NASA's Aeolus atmospheric instruments and doubling as a c  Space Daily
  2. NASA Announces Public-Private Partnership to Advance Mars Science  NASA (.gov)
  3. NASA picks Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for Mars mission, setting up a race with SpaceX  TechCrunch
  4. Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission  SpaceNews
  5. NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars  The Verge

  5. The Pink Planet is so weird, astronomers struggle to define it. And they've just found it's covered in salty clouds  BBC Sky at Night Magazine

item.title: Powerful seismic waves from Japan's 2011 earthquake struck Earth's core and bounced back up, moving the island eastward - Phys.org

item.title: The American flags planted by Apollo astronauts have likely bleached pure white after 50 years of unfiltered solar radiation, leaving the most famous banners in history standing as blank cloth - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The American flags planted by Apollo astronauts have likely bleached pure white after 50 years of unfiltered solar radiation, leaving the most famous banners in history standing as blank cloth  Space Daily
  2. Apollo 11's flag was knocked flat by the lunar module's own exhaust the instant the astronauts lifted off, meaning the first banner on the moon stood upright for less than a day  Space Daily
  3. Apollo astronauts planted American flags on the Moon- 50 years of unfiltered sunlight may have bleached them into blank white cloth  The Times of India

item.title: A lunar meteorite found in northwest Africa has been shown to record evidence of an asteroid impact on the Moon 3.5 billion years ago, matching independently dated impacts on Earth and on the asteroid 4 Vesta from the same period, at the moment life on Ea - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A lunar meteorite found in northwest Africa has been shown to record evidence of an asteroid impact on the Moon 3.5 billion years ago, matching independently dated impacts on Earth and on the asteroid 4 Vesta from the same period, at the moment life on Ea  Space Daily
  2. A 3.5-billion-year-old Moon meteorite found in Africa reveals a hidden chapter of lunar history  The Times of India
  3. These nearly invisible crystals could rewrite what we know about the early Solar System  The Economic Times
  4. Moon Impact 3.5 Billion Years Ago Hints at Hidden Era of Solar System  ScienceAlert
  5. A Piece of the Moon Found in Africa Records a Giant Lunar Impact From the Dawn of Life on Earth  ZME Science

item.title: Some climate models suggest Venus could once have had liquid water and habitable temperatures, until a dramatic transformation hundreds of millions of years ago. One leading idea is that widespread volcanic resurfacing helped push the planet into the run - Space Daily

  3. Scientists Propose That Entire Universes Can Form Inside Collapsing Stars  Futurism
  4. A dying star might never collapse into a black hole — a tiny Big Bang could ignite at its core and leave a gravastar with no singularity and no event horizon  Space Daily
  5. Gravastars: Not all dying stars end up as black holes  Earth.com

item.title: 344 steps stood between the James Webb Space Telescope and total failure — any one could have ended it — and the telescope that survived them all now runs on less power than a household kettle, a million miles from Earth - Space Daily
item.description: 344 steps stood between the James Webb Space Telescope and total failure — any one could have ended it — and the telescope that survived them all now runs on less power than a household kettle, a million miles from Earth  Space Daily

item.title: Charles Darwin argued that the roots of human morality were not separate from animal life, and the surprise is what that does to civilization — manners, conscience, and restraint become not the opposite of instinct, but instinct complicated by memory, languag - Space Daily
item.description: Charles Darwin argued that the roots of human morality were not separate from animal life, and the surprise is what that does to civilization — manners, conscience, and restraint become not the opposite of instinct, but instinct complicated by memory, languag  Space Daily

item.title: Whoever finds the Voyager Golden Record will know exactly how long it has been drifting, because the scientists who built it pressed a small sample of uranium into the cover — a built-in clock that keeps time for roughly a billion years. - Space Daily
item.description: Whoever finds the Voyager Golden Record will know exactly how long it has been drifting, because the scientists who built it pressed a small sample of uranium into the cover — a built-in clock that keeps time for roughly a billion years.  Space Daily
